name: Test-infra: assess seedTrialExpiredChild + seedConsentPending sub-13 birthYear (else /v1/profiles 500 if signed in as parent)
description: af79 (WI-799) adversarial-review residual finding. The 3 LEAVE seed sites in apps/api/src/services/test-seed.ts keep birthYear:2014 (sub-13). 2 of them — seedTrialExpiredChild (paywall/state-machine driven) and seedConsentPending (consentRequest-row driven) — may NOT actually need sub-13 for what they test. If a suite (e.g. mentor-audit-registry-smoke) signs those scenarios in AS PARENT and loads GET /v1/profiles, the v1 birthYearSchema >=13 read-floor 500s the whole family list (same mechanism as WI-799). OFF-LANE: does NOT gate 586/the flip (the affected suite is separate from the auth.setup gating smoke). Source: af79 PR #1205 comment; ic-orch-038 ruling 1.
acceptanceCriteria: 1. For seedTrialExpiredChild and seedConsentPending: determine whether the child sub-13 birthYear is actually required by what the seed exercises. 2. If age is IRRELEVANT (trial/paywall state-driven; consent-pending consentRequest-row driven) -> bump to >=13 via CHILD_BIRTH_YEAR (test-data fix, mirrors WI-799). 3. If genuinely NEEDS sub-13 (to exercise an under-threshold consent gate) -> concrete instance of the C2/AC#6 v1.1 read-floor landmine -> do NOT bump; fold/link into the compliance-owned v1.1 item. 4. Verify the 3rd LEAVE site (seedMentorAuditPostApprovalRedirect, line 4594) under the same lens. 5. After any bumps: confirm GET /v1/profiles no longer 500s when signed in as parent for the affected scenarios.
riskImpact: E2E suites signing in as parent and calling GET /v1/profiles 500 due to v1 birthYearSchema >=13 read-floor rejecting sub-13 child profiles seeded by these 3 LEAVE sites. OFF-LANE: does not gate WI-586 flip.
